content.problemStatement:
The greatest common non-divisor of two positive integers XXX and YYY satisfying X‚â•5X \ge 5X‚â•5 and Y‚â•5Y \ge 5Y‚â•5, denoted gcnd(X,Y)\text{gcnd}(X, Y)gcnd(X,Y), is defined to be the largest positive integer ZZZ satisfying the following conditions:


Z‚â§XZ \le XZ‚â§X **or** Z‚â§YZ \le YZ‚â§Y, and
XXX is not a multiple of ZZZ **and** YYY is not a multiple of ZZZ.
It can be proved that as long as XXX and YYY are both ‚â•5\ge 5‚â•5, gcnd(X,Y)\text{gcnd}(X, Y)gcnd(X,Y) always exists.

You are given an array AAA of length NNN, such that 5‚â§Ai‚â§1075 \le A_i \le 10^75‚â§Ai‚Äã‚â§107 for each 1‚â§i‚â§N1 \le i \le N1‚â§i‚â§N. Your task is to compute the maximum gcnd\text{gcnd}gcnd of some two elements of the array.

That is, across all pairs of indices (i,j)(i, j)(i,j) satisfying 1‚â§i<j‚â§N1 \le i \lt j \le N1‚â§i<j‚â§N, compute the maximum possible value of gcnd(Ai,Aj)\text{gcnd}(A_i, A_j)gcnd(Ai‚Äã,Aj‚Äã).


Input Format

The first line of input will contain a single integer TTT, denoting the number of test cases.
Each test case consists of two lines of input.

The first line of each test case contains a single integer NNN ‚Äî the length of the array.
The second line contains NNN space-separated integers A1,A2,‚Ä¶,ANA_1, A_2, \ldots, A_NA1‚Äã,A2‚Äã,‚Ä¶,AN‚Äã ‚Äî the elements of the array.




Output Format
For each test case, output on a new line the answer: the maximum possible value of gcnd(Ai,Aj)\text{gcnd}(A_i, A_j)gcnd(Ai‚Äã,Aj‚Äã) across all 1‚â§i<j‚â§N1 \le i \lt j \le N1‚â§i<j‚â§N.
constraints: The sum of NNN over all test cases won't exceed 2‚ãÖ1052\cdot 10^52‚ãÖ105.

sampleTestCases.explanation:
Explanation:
Test case 111: The only choice is to compute gcnd(8,5)\text{gcnd}(8, 5)gcnd(8,5), since there are only two elements in the array.
It can be verified that gcnd(8,5)=7\text{gcnd}(8, 5) = 7gcnd(8,5)=7, because 777 divides neither 555 nor 888, and no larger integer has this property.
Test case 222: All the elements are equal to 666, so the answer is gcnd(6,6)\text{gcnd}(6, 6)gcnd(6,6), which can be verified to be 555.
Test case 333: One optimal choice is to choose i=1i = 1i=1 and j=4j = 4j=4, to obtain gcnd(A1,A4)=gcnd(8,8)=7\text{gcnd}(A_1, A_4) = \text{gcnd}(8, 8) = 7gcnd(A1‚Äã,A4‚Äã)=gcnd(8,8)=7.
It can be proved that no other pair of elements in the array has a larger gcnd\text{gcnd}gcnd value.
